Manchester United fastest player

Premier League statisticians Opta have clocked the five fastest speeds recorded by United players so far this season and a defender has muscled his way into the list.

5. Henrikh Mkhitaryan - 33.68km/h

Mkhitaryan has spent the season in the number 10 role where a source claims he is at his 'most dangerous' and the Armenian has started his second season with United quicker than his first.

If Mkhitaryan claims another assist at Stoke this weekend he will become the third player in the Premier League era to have accumulated six assists in his first four appearances.

The United playmaker has already tallied five assists in three league games - as many as he ended last season with - after creating two goals against West Ham and Swansea before crossing for Rashford's winner against Leicester two weeks ago.
Mkhitaryan has thrived in the number 10 role

4. Phil Jones - 33.8km/h

Jones has started the season superbly and was nominated for the Premier League Player of the Month award after a mixed first season under Mourinho. Jones performed well at centre half but regularly succumbed to injury.

"It's weird, I feel like I have still done a lot at United, I still feel like have developed as a player," he said last week. "I feel like I've grown into myself, if you like, and I've seen a lot of faces come and go.

"Some people say in pre-season 'he might go here, he might be going there', then you look at all the faces that have gone and you think to yourself 'I can't be doing too bad if I am still here at the best club, one of the best clubs in the world?'"
Jones has started the season superbly

3. Marcus Rashford - 34.27km/h

Rashford broke his duck for the campaign with the winner against Leicester, having shone without scoring in the wins over West Ham and Swansea.

Mourinho has primarily played the Wythenshawe teenager on the left flank over the last year and Rashford could return there at Stoke after receiving the man of the match award on international duty with England in midweek.

Gareth Southgate inexplicably started Rashford on the right but switched him to the left and he netted the World Cup qualifying winner against Slovakia.

2. Romelu Lukaku - 34.56km/h

Lukaku made it eight goals in six games for club and country against Greece in Piraeus earlier this week and his pace has enhanced a United attack which often resorted to long balls with Ibrahimovic in the side.

The Belgian's willingness to move into the channels and offer a more mobile outlet than United's former number nine has encouraged his fellow forwards to be intuitive with their movement, and Lukaku finishes just behind his friend in the United speed list.
Lukaku's pace has made a big difference

1. Paul Pogba - 34.68 km/h

Pogba was a surprise omission from the Premier League's Player of the Month shortlist but he received the United equivalent, having earned two man of the match awards from supporters against Swansea and Leicester.

The France international did not claim his second goal of last season until mid-October but he has hit that figure already as he attempts to reach double figures for the campaign.

Mourinho said in February Pogba's £89million fee would prove to be cheap and it looks like he was right.
